title: senecio longiflorus subsp. madagascariensis
intro: Senecio longiflorus subsp. madagascariensis, also known as the Madagascar ragwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Senecio longiflorus subsp. madagascariensis include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and protecting against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be made by drying the leaves and flowers, which are then used to create medicinal products.
